data.title: Coinbase Wallet Unleashes Built-In Mini Apps in Limited Beta Phase
data.description:
TL;DR Integrated Mini Apps: Coinbase Wallet now features built-in mini apps that allow users to access dApps directly from the wallet, eliminating the need for extra browsers. Seamless Web3 Interactions: With a simple tap on listings or shared links, users can instantly engage in activities like minting NFTs, trading tokens, or using DeFi protocols. Future-Ready. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/coinbase-wallet-unleashes-built-in-mini-apps-in-limited-beta-phase/

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Taps DeFi for Liquidity, Borrows $2M
data.description:
The move was revealed by Marc Zeller, founder of the Aavechan Initiative.
source: https://coindoo.com/ethereum-foundation-taps-defi-for-liquidity-borrows-2m/

data.title: UK Reform Party Leader Nigel Farage Follows Trump's Example and Plans to Make Britain a Cryptocurrency Hub
Viewpoint: The Fed’s hawkish stance triggered a short-term pullback, but BTC’s upward trend may continue
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Although the Fed's meeting minutes showed a hawkish stance that led to a brief market correction, analysts expect Bitcoin and cryptocurrency markets to resume their recent upward trend. Analysts pointed out that although the Fed may maintain high interest rates, Bitcoin's upward momentum remains strong, mainly benefiting from the continued interest of institutional investors, progress in stablecoin regulation, and concerns about the declining attractiveness of US dollar assets. In addition, recent data showed that inflows into US cryptocurrency ETFs hit a new high since January, indicating that institutional participation is deepening. Analysts believe that although there may be fluctuations in the short term, the fundamentals of the crypto market remain solid in the long run.
(The Block)

data.title: Arbitrum Launches ArbiFuel, a Gas Fee Sponsorship Program
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Ethereum's second-layer network Arbitrum announced the launch of the Gas fee sponsorship program ArbiFuel, which aims to help early teams deliver faster, test freely, and allow users to not worry about Gas fees. It is reported that ArbiFuel will last for three months, from May 30 to August 30, 2025, and will be open to eligible Arbitrum development teams.

data.title: Circle's IPO Progress May Lead to Canceled Acquisition Plans
data.description: According to Odaily, the anticipated initial public offering (IPO) of stablecoin company Circle is progressing smoothly. Jay Woods, Chief Global Strategist at Freedom Capital Markets, noted that with the IPO's pricing and timeline already established, the initial acquisition plans might be canceled. Woods added that only an extraordinary offer could potentially halt the IPO by completing an acquisition before the company goes public.

data.title: Japanese Prime Minister Considers U.S. Visit Before G7 Summit
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba held a 25-minute phone conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump. Additionally, Prime Minister Ishiba indicated the possibility of visiting the United States before the upcoming G7 Summit, should the need arise.

data.title: The father of the World Wide Web: DNS should be more decentralized
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Tim Berners-Lee, the inventor of the World Wide Web and Hypertext Markup Language (HTML), said that if he built the Domain Name Service (DNS) today, it would make it "more decentralized." Tim Berners-Lee believes that the current high degree of centralization of DNS, especially in the management of top-level domain names, makes it vulnerable to censorship and control. For example, the root domain name is managed by the US non-profit organization ICANN, and the most authoritative servers are also operated by a few organizations. This centralization brings potential censorship and privacy issues. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation has staked 50,000 RAIL on the chain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to on-chain data, the Ethereum Foundation pledged 50,000 RAILs, about $50,000, 15 hours ago. The Ethereum Foundation can now participate in Railgun governance and receive active governance rewards from protocol fees.

data.title: Opinion: Stablecoin company Circle is unlikely to be acquired before IPO
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - The IPO of stablecoin company Circle is highly anticipated. Jay Woods, chief global strategist at Freedom Capital Markets, said that due to the smooth progress of the IPO, for example, the price has been determined and the timetable has been given, so its original plan to be acquired may be cancelled. Jay Woods added: "Unless someone makes a ridiculous offer to prevent the IPO from happening, the acquisition transaction will be completed before they go public." (Blockworks)

data.title: Tim Berners-Lee Advocates for a Decentralized DNS System
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Tim Berners-Lee, the inventor of the World Wide Web and hypertext markup language (HTML), expressed his views on the current structure of the Domain Name Service (DNS) during a discussion at ETH Prague. Speaking alongside Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin, Berners-Lee reflected on the early decisions made regarding internet architecture. He stated that if he were to build a DNS today, he would opt for a more decentralized approach. The DNS, developed in 1983 by Paul Mockapetris, replaced the previous hosts.txt file-based system with a scalable, distributed system for naming IP addresses. Although Berners-Lee was not directly involved in the development of DNS, he acknowledged that he could have influenced the URL structure to be less dependent on the centralized DNS system or advocated for a decentralized alternative.

The current DNS system is notably centralized, especially at its top levels. The root zone, which manages top-level domains like .com and .org, is controlled by the U.S.-based nonprofit Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN). Additionally, the most authoritative servers are operated by a limited number of organizations, further centralizing the system. This centralization allows for potential censorship and control by governments, which can unilaterally seize or block domain names. The centralized nature of DNS also increases the likelihood of outages due to service disruptions. Control over root zones and domain policies is concentrated in a few regions and among select stakeholders, raising concerns about privacy as centralized DNS queries can be monitored, logged, and intercepted by internet service providers and other intermediaries.

In response to these issues, decentralized alternatives to the DNS system are being developed. The Ethereum Name Service (ENS) is one such alternative, mapping human-readable names to Ethereum addresses, content on the Inter-Planetary File System (IPFS), or other data. Unstoppable Domains offers another solution by minting domains as non-fungible tokens (NFTs) on Ethereum and Polygon. ENS has gained traction, and in late 2024, ENS Labs partnered with PayPal and Venmo, sparking speculation about increased mainstream adoption. IPFS, which runs on Ethereum smart contracts, aims to be user-owned and censorship-resistant, primarily used in Web3 applications, wallets, and decentralized websites. Handshake (HNS) and Namecoin (NMC) are other projects aiming to provide decentralized and permissionless naming protocols compatible with traditional DNS. OpenNIC, a non-crypto alternative, is a user-run project offering non-ICANN domains, seeking to decentralize control through community governance.

data.title: IBIT's Low Volatility Attracts Major Investors
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bloomberg Senior ETF Analyst Eric Balchunas recently noted on the X platform that IBIT's low volatility increase is drawing significant investment. The 90-day rolling volatility of IBIT is decreasing and has reached unprecedented lows. This combination of low volatility and price increase is appealing to large investors who prefer 'digital gold' over tech stocks.

This trend explains why IBIT has seen higher fund inflows over the past six weeks compared to the average levels of other ETFs.

data.title: ETH/USDT,BTC/USDT and SOL/USDT Lead Binance USD-M Perpetual Futures Market in Trading Volume Over the Past 24 Hours
data.description: According to Binance Futures data, over the past 24 hours, the ETH/USDT, BTC/USDT, SOL/USDT, 1000PEPE/USDT and SOPH/USDT trading pairs led the USD-M perpetual futures market in trading volume.ETH/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 1.64, funding rate: 0.0045%.BTC/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 1.05, funding rate: 0.0100%.SOL/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 3.01, funding rate: 0.0082%.1000PEPE/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 2.42, funding rate: 0.0100%.SOPH/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 0.95, funding rate: -0.2381%.

data.title: Spot Gold Prices Surge Amid U.S. Court Tariff Ruling Developments
data.description: According to PANews, spot gold prices have seen a significant increase, reaching $3,320 per ounce, marking a 1.02% rise within the day. This surge comes as several U.S. officials express confidence that a recent court ruling on tariffs will be overturned, suggesting a strong likelihood of a favorable outcome for the government.

data.title: Binance Japan Bags Major ISO Security Certifications
data.description:
The Japanese arm of top crypto exchange Binance was recently awarded the ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27701 certifications.
source: https://www.coinspeaker.com/binance-japan-bags-major-iso-security-certifications/

data.title: 5 signs that suggest Bitcoin price has 'room for further expansion'
data.description:
Key takeaways:Bitcoin’s price consolidates below its all-time high of nearly $112,000. Whale accumulation, strong ETF inflows, and other factors suggest BTC is on track to $120,000. Bitcoin (BTC) shows multiple onchain and technical signals that there is still more upside for BTC. Bitcoin whales accumulate more BTCLarge Bitcoin investors have been adding to their holdings in anticipation of price increases in the future
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/5-bitcoin-signs-btc-price-rally-room-for-further-expansion?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bloomberg analyst: BlackRock Bitcoin ETF volatility is falling, which may attract large investors
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that the 90-day rolling volatility of BlackRock's Bitcoin exchange-traded fund IBIT is declining and has almost hit a low. This trend seems to be consistent with the higher price of Bitcoin. This trend helps attract larger investors (who want digital gold, not technology stocks), and also explains why IBIT's fund flow capture is much higher than the average level of the past six weeks.

data.title: Blockchain Builders announces $28 million in Fund I
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Blockchain Builders, a blockchain venture fund with Stanford University background, announced that its Fund I has completed $28 million in fundraising. The fund was founded by Stanford University graduate students Gil Rosen, Kun Peng and Steven Willinger. It has invested more than $16 million in 40 blockchain companies, covering artificial intelligence, infrastructure, Defi, DePIN, payment and RWA. The remaining funds are expected to be deployed to other blockchain projects by the end of the year. (Chainwire)

data.title: BNB Chain Launches Exclusive Incentive Program for Real-World Asset (RWA) Projects
data.description: BNB Chain has announced the launch of an exclusive Real-World Asset (RWA) track incentive program. The program aims to provide comprehensive, customized support for RWA projects that are building on or migrating to BNB Chain, accelerating RWA adoption and empowering project growth. Support includes, but is not limited to: technical and compliance guidance,  liquidity seeding and TVL incentives, growth-focused funding and marketing, as well as customized roadmaps for scalability.Applications will open on May 29 and remain open on a rolling basis. Once submitted, projects will undergo a multi-dimensional evaluation process, with high-quality RWA projects eligible for incentive support. Applications are not limited to any specific RWA sub-sector. Projects are also encouraged to integrate with the DeFiLlama platform to enhance visibility and accessibility for users.

data.title: Solana Ecosystem Blockchain Payment Network Zebec Network Acquires Science Card
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Zebec Network, a blockchain payment network based on Solana, has acquired Science Card, a secure and transparent financial tool for students, researchers and university faculty. This acquisition enriches Zebec's powerful card product line and may expand its crypto debit card and other products. (Chainwire)
